directives.name: TooltipDirective
directives.description:
Shows a styled tooltip (the design-system .nao-tooltip) on hover. By default it only appears when the host's text is truncated, which makes it ideal for ellipsised labels (e.g. a work-order name in a narrow bar).

Usage: <span class="..." [naoTooltip]="name()">{{ name() }}</span>
directives.rawdescription: Shows a styled tooltip (the design-system `.nao-tooltip`) on hover. By default it only appears when the host's text is truncated, which makes it ideal for ellipsised labels (e.g. a work-order name in a narrow bar). Usage: `{{ name() }}`

inputsClass.name: debounceMs
inputsClass.description: Delay in ms before the tooltip appears on hover (default 0 = instant).
inputsClass.rawdescription: Delay in ms before the tooltip appears on hover (default 0 = instant).

inputsClass.name: onlyWhenTruncated
inputsClass.description: Only show when the host content is overflowing (default true).
inputsClass.rawdescription: Only show when the host content is overflowing (default true).

inputsClass.name: position
inputsClass.rawdescription: Tooltip placement relative to the host.

methodsClass.name: scheduleShow
methodsClass.rawdescription: Show after the configured debounce; a 0 delay shows immediately.
methodsClass.description: Show after the configured debounce; a 0 delay shows immediately.

components.name: IconButtonComponent
components.templateData:
<button
  class="nao-icon-btn"
  [class]="'nao-icon-btn--' + variant()"
  type="button"
  [attr.aria-label]="label()"
  (click)="clicked.emit()"
>
  @if (variant() === 'dismiss') {
    <svg width="10" height="10" viewBox="0 0 10 10" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
      <path d="M1 1L9 9M9 1L1 9" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    </svg>
  } @else {
    <svg width="16" height="4" viewBox="0 0 16 4" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
      <circle cx="2" cy="2" r="2" fill="currentColor"/>
      <circle cx="8" cy="2" r="2" fill="currentColor"/>
      <circle cx="14" cy="2" r="2" fill="currentColor"/>
    </svg>
  }
</button>

propertiesClass.rawdescription: Free pixel offset of the hover "add" pill (follows the cursor), or null.

variables.name: MONTH_SECTIONS
variables.rawdescription: Each month is divided into this many equal placement sections ("weeks").
variables.description: Each month is divided into this many equal placement sections ("weeks").

variables.name: WORK_CENTERS
variables.defaultValue:
[
  { docId: 'wc-1', docType: 'workCenter', data: { name: 'Extrusion Line A' } },
  { docId: 'wc-2', docType: 'workCenter', data: { name: 'CNC Machine 1' } },
  { docId: 'wc-3', docType: 'workCenter', data: { name: 'Assembly Station' } },
  { docId: 'wc-4', docType: 'workCenter', data: { name: 'Quality Control' } },
  { docId: 'wc-5', docType: 'workCenter', data: { name: 'Packaging Line' } },
]
variables.rawdescription:
Seed data for the Work Order Schedule.

Demonstrates the spec requirements:
- 5 work centers with realistic manufacturing names
- 9 work orders across them, covering all 4 statuses
- Several centers carry multiple, non-overlapping orders (wc-1, wc-3, wc-4)
- Multi-month spans so every bar is legible even at Month zoom, all within the
  visible range (timeline starts one month before "today" = 2026-06-09)
variables.description:
Seed data for the Work Order Schedule.

Demonstrates the spec requirements:

  • 5 work centers with realistic manufacturing names
  • 9 work orders across them, covering all 4 statuses
  • Several centers carry multiple, non-overlapping orders (wc-1, wc-3, wc-4)
  • Multi-month spans so every bar is legible even at Month zoom, all within the visible range (timeline starts one month before "today" = 2026-06-09)

variables.name: WORK_ORDERS

functions.name: dateToOffset
functions.description: Pixel offset of the slot that contains date (Month → section, Week/Day → cell).

functions.name: findCurrentCellLeft
functions.description: Pixel offset of the cell that contains current, or null if out of range.

functions.name: offsetToDateRange
functions.description: Inclusive date range represented by a placement slot at left.

functions.name: placeBar
functions.description: Left/width of a work-order bar. Both ends snap to whole slots, and the end date is inclusive — the bar covers the slot its end date falls in.

functions.name: slotWidthFor
functions.description: Pixel width of one placement slot for the given scale.

functions.name: snapPlacementLeft
functions.description: Snaps a free pixel offset to a valid placement slot for the given scale: Month → one of 4 equal sections inside the month, Week / Day → the whole cell.

functions.name: timelineStartBefore
functions.description: ISO date one slot before the period containing current (for the timeline start).

typealiases.name: WorkOrderStatus
typealiases.description:
Document models for the Work Order Schedule.

Every document follows the ERP envelope { docId, docType, data } described in the technical spec, so this shape mirrors what a real Naologic API would return.
